A standout shoyu ramen spot in Tokyo. Their signature menu is the “Special Shoyu Ramen.” Built on a chicken-based broth blended with multiple soy sauces, the first sip brings out the richness of chicken oil, deep umami from the soy sauce, and a subtle sweetness. As you continue, the flavors finish clean and balanced. The charcoal-grilled chashu adds a gentle smoky aroma that naturally melts into the broth✨ The straight noodles hold the soup well, delivering its flavor all the way through each bite. The chashu comes in a variety of cuts, each prepared differently—from rich, fatty pieces to lighter, more delicate textures—letting you enjoy a contrast of juiciness and mouthfeel. [Ramen Sakurai 麺屋 さくら井] - Location: 2 Chome-15-27 Kamirenjaku, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Hours: Mon & Sat 11:30 - 15:00 / 18:00 - 20:30, Tue/Thu/Fri 11:30 - 15:00, Sun 11:30 - 16:00 (Closed Wed) - Menu: Special Shoyu Ramen (¥2,000) #ramen #tokyoramen #shoyuramen #라멘

Are they really back? Yes, absolutely. We exclusively tested both new Mercurial models in Madrid (thanks to @Nike Football and @hubcommnet), as always, we want to give you every possible detail to build your own opinion: visuals, product insights, but most importantly our real feedback after using them on pitch, in matches and during specific training sessions that seriously “stressed” both boots. The new Superfly sends a very clear message as soon as you put it on: innovation built to deliver maximum propulsion. That’s exactly what the forefoot “plate” gives you, an aggressive sense of push and energy return that you can genuinely feel while sprinting. Vapor changes too and the almost “strange” feeling when wearing it for the first time is actually maximum comfort. Not because Mercurial Vapor has never been comfortable, but comfort has probably never been the first thing that comes to mind when thinking about this boot. Ultra Creamy Buldak Ramen 🍝🥟 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1–2 tbsp butter 2 cloves garlic (minced) 1 tbsp Kewpie mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet 1 tbsp chili oil ¾–1 cup heavy cream Mozzarella cheese Dumplings Sausages Green onions Sesame seeds Extra chili oil (for topping) Instructions Cook noodles until al dente, then drain and set aside. In a pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant. Add Kewpie mayo, Buldak sauce, and powder packet. Mix well until it turns thick and glossy. Add chili oil and stir. Pour in heavy cream and mix until smooth, creamy, and slightly thickened (that rich orange color 🔥). Add noodles and toss until fully coated. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top and let it melt into the sauce. Add dumplings and sausages on top. Cover and let everything heat through. Finish with green onions, sesame seeds, and extra chili oil drizzle. . #buldak #buldakramen #eat #cook #Foodie

Dumbbell Sumo Squat  Know the difference  1. Dumbbell Position Hold the dumbbell with both hands naturally hanging between your legs, rather than holding it at your chest. This allows you to lift heavier weights without being limited by arm strength. 2. Torso Posture Maintain a slight forward lean instead of staying fully upright. Leaning forward increases hip flexion, helping to stretch and better activate the glutes. 3. Stance Width and Foot Angle Set your feet about 1.5 times shoulder-width apart, with your toes pointing outward at roughly a 45-degree angle. If the stance is too wide, the inner thigh muscles (adductors) will take over, reducing glute activation. 4. Load Path As you squat down, the dumbbell should not stay directly under your shoulders. Instead, it should fall slightly behind the midline, closer to your posterior chain. Bring your hands slightly inward to keep the load closer to your center of mass, which helps reduce lower back strain and improves movement efficiency. #gymtips #quads #gluteworkout #gym #gymrat

Raising Cane’s-Style Chicken Fingers With Cane’s Sauce. Ingredients: For the Chicken: - 2 lb chicken breast, cut into strips - Oil for frying For the Wet Batter: - 2 cups buttermilk - 2 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tbsp black pepper - Salt to taste - 1 tbsp smoked paprika For the Dry Batter: - 2 cups all-purpose flour - 3 tbsp cornstarch - Salt to taste - 1 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tsp black pepper - 1 tsp smoked paprika For the Cane’s Sauce: - 1 cup mayonnaise - 4 tbsp ketchup - 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce - 1 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tsp black pepper - Salt to taste - 1 tsp smoked paprika Instructions: 1. Prepare the wet batter by whisking together buttermilk, garlic powder, black pepper, salt, and smoked paprika. Add the chicken strips to the wet batter and marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight. 2. Prepare the dry batter by whisking together flour, cornstarch, salt, garlic powder, black pepper, and smoked paprika. 3. Remove the chicken from the wet batter, allowing excess to drip off, then coat each strip in the dry batter. 4. Deep-fry the coated chicken strips in hot oil (about 350°F) until golden brown and cooked through, approximately 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through. 5. Prepare the Cane’s Sauce by whisking together mayonnaise, ketchup,Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, black pepper, salt, and smoked paprika. 6. Serve the crispy chicken fingers with Cane’s Sauce. #canessauce #raisingcanes #friedchicken #chickentenders #Recipe
